title: Patriots to reportedly open 2026 season with Super Bowl LX rematch vs. Seahawks
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The New England Patriots will open the 2026 NFL season against the Seattle Seahawks in a Super Bowl LX rematch.
The game will be played on September 9 at Seattle's Lumen Field and broadcast on NBC.
The Patriots will face the Detroit Lions in Munich, Germany on November 15 as part of the international games schedule.

title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-0 loss to Nashville SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The New England Revolution's seven-game unbeaten run ended with a 3-0 defeat to Nashville SC at Gillette Stadium.
Nashville's Bryan Acosta scored twice, with Warren Madrigal adding another, capitalizing on a mistake by goalkeeper Matt Turner.
New England, now third in the Eastern Conference, faces Minnesota United next amid fixture congestion and lineup evaluations.

title: Massport to attempt Guinness World Record for world’s largest soccer ball
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Massport will attempt a Guinness World Record with a 45-foot-tall soccer ball at Piers Park II in East Boston.
The current world record for the largest soccer ball is 38 feet and was set in Doha in 2013.
The soccer ball will be displayed from June 12-18, coinciding with FIFA World Cup events at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough.

title: Famous makeup artist gets backlash for mocking former Spirit Airlines employee at Logan over fundraiser
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: James Charles criticized a former Spirit Airlines employee who lost her job after the airline's sudden shutdown.
Amber Lendof Vargas, affected by Spirit Airlines' closure, launched a $35,000 GoFundMe for financial assistance.
Charles later apologized, donated $5,000 to the fundraiser, and faced backlash from fellow influencers.

title: Photos: State trooper killed by wrong-way driver in Lynnfield crash laid to rest
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Trooper Kevin Trainor, killed by a wrong-way driver, was laid to rest in Salem on May 13, 2026.
Governor Maura Healey attended and spoke at the funeral service held at St. James Church in Salem.
The crash that killed Trainor also resulted in the death of the wrong-way driver, Hernan Marrero, on May 6.

title: Angell Animal Medical Center treating cat who fell 10 stories
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Angell Animal Medical Center in Boston is treating a cat, Mazy, who survived a fall from a 15th floor window.
Mazy suffered two broken paws and a collapsed lung requiring surgery estimated to cost up to $15,000.
MSPCA-Angell is investigating the circumstances of the fall and soliciting donations for Mazy's medical expenses.

title: AG says auditor can hire attorney to force audit of Legislature after SJC order
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Attorney General Andrea Campbell approved Auditor Diana DiZoglio's request to hire an attorney for a legislative audit.
The Supreme Judicial Court ordered a 30-day deadline for the AG to decide on DiZoglio's audit request.
DiZoglio aims to audit Senate finances for fiscal years 2021-2024, focusing on budgets, audits, transactions, and agreements.

title: Kyle Schwarber reveals tepid pursuit from Red Sox this past offseason
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kyle Schwarber revealed minimal offseason discussions with the Red Sox before re-signing with the Phillies on a five-year, $150 million deal.
Schwarber, who hit 204 home runs in five seasons with the Phillies, leads MLB with 17 home runs in the 2026 season.
Schwarber's career OPS of 1.137 at Fenway Park ranks fifth all-time among players with at least 100 plate appearances at the historic venue.

title: Jayson Tatum reveals new goal he’s chasing with the Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum aims to win another NBA championship and earn the Finals MVP title with the Celtics in 2026-27.
Tatum returned in March from a ruptured Achilles tendon but faced knee soreness, missing a critical playoff game.
Despite injury challenges, Tatum is optimistic about a full offseason of training to restore his performance levels.

title: 7 contracts Bruins might try to unload this offseason
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins have $16 million in cap space but plan to offload contracts for more financial flexibility.
Goaltender Joonas Korpisalo and forward Casey Mittelstadt are potential trade candidates to clear salary cap space.
Elias Lindholm's $7.75 million contract is a challenge, with concerns over injury and trade complexity.
